rationale: The two papers are tightly aligned in domain and constructs: the citing study models retweet/content-sharing on Twitter, while the cited study provides causal evidence on tweets and influential retweets as a marketing tool (both social-media rebroadcasting/retweet behavior). Given this close topical overlap and the cited paper’s potential role as a foundational empirical benchmark/mechanism source, this is not safely an obvious incidental mention despite only a DOI-level match being provided.
